theorem_content.abstract: geometric_tail_bound: for 0 ≤ r < 1 and c ≥ 0, ∑ c * r^n = c / (1 - r). k_tail_convergence: if |step K| ≤ C * r^K for all K, then step is summable.
theorem_content.physical_meaning: The monotone K-tail extrapolation uses the geometric series formula ∑ delta_K8 * r^n = delta_K8 / (1 - r) as the tail bound. This formula is machine-verified to be exact (given the geometric convergence assumption). The certificate honestly reports whether the resulting bound meets the target.
theorem_content.certificate_backing: The KTailBoundProof module provides the mathematical basis for claiming the geometric tail extrapolation formula is exact. The finite certificate computation (which failed the target) is an honest application of this verified formula.
